title: End-to-end validation against live hegel-core server
description: Validate all four conjectures (C-001 through C-004) against a live hegel-core 0.2.2 server. Run examples/basic.scm end-to-end. Capture wire traffic to confirm framing and handshake behavior.
close_reason: EVV complete. Major finding: hegel-core 0.2.2 protocol is entirely different from conjectures. C-001 REFUTED (HEGL packet framing, not uint32-BE), C-002 REFUTED (raw bytes handshake, not CBOR), C-003 CONFIRMED (snake_case keys), C-004 PARTIALLY REFUTED (command/event pattern, Status enum). Schema type names also wrong (plural→singular). Filed hegel-guile-2 through hegel-guile-7 for remediation.

title: C-001 REFUTED: Wire format is HEGL packet framing, not uint32-BE length prefix
description: hegel-core 0.2.2 uses a structured packet format: [magic 0x4845474C][CRC32][channel_id][message_id][payload_length][payload][0x0A terminator]. Not simple length-prefixed CBOR. cbor.scm cbor-encode-to-port and cbor-decode-from-port must be replaced with packet-level framing.
close_reason: Implemented in packet.scm (HEGL framing), crc32.scm (checksum). Old cbor-encode-to-port/cbor-decode-from-port removed. 19 packet tests + 7 CRC32 tests pass. Verified against live hegel-core 0.2.3 via stdio-probe.scm.

title: C-002 REFUTED: Handshake is raw bytes, not CBOR message
description: hegel-core 0.2.2 handshake: client sends raw b'hegel_handshake_start' on channel 0, server replies b'Hegel/0.7'. Not the CBOR {type: handshake, client: ..., version: ...} format.
close_reason: Implemented in protocol.scm (%handshake-string, parse-server-version) and server.scm (perform-handshake). Verified against live hegel-core 0.2.3: handshake completes, Hegel/0.7 returned.

title: C-004 PARTIAL REFUTATION: Protocol uses command/event pattern, not type-based messages
description: hegel-core uses {command: generate/mark_complete/run_test} and {event: test_case/test_done}. Status uses Hypothesis Status enum (VALID/INVALID/INTERESTING). Not the {type: draw/finish_test_case, status: passed/failed/invalid} format.
close_reason: protocol.scm rewritten with msg-run-test, msg-generate, msg-assume, msg-mark-complete. Response accessors updated for command/event keys. 29 protocol tests pass. Note: live probe revealed run_test needs channel_id at top level and status strings must be UPPERCASE — see hegel-guile-8, hegel-guile-9.

title: Schema type names don't match hegel-core: integers→integer, booleans→boolean, etc.
description: generators.scm uses plural type names (integers, booleans, floats, text, lists) but hegel-core schema.py expects singular (integer, boolean, float, string, list). Live probe confirmed: {type: integers} returned 'Unsupported schema'. Need to verify actual schema vocabulary against hegel-core from_schema().

title: Implement HEGL packet framing layer (packet.scm)
description: New module src/hegel/packet.scm implementing: read-packet/write-packet with magic cookie 0x4845474C, CRC32 checksum, channel_id, message_id with reply bit, payload length, payload, 0x0A terminator.
close_reason: Implemented: src/hegel/packet.scm (write-hegl-packet!, read-hegl-packet!), src/hegel/crc32.scm. 19 packet tests + 7 CRC32 tests pass. Wire-compatible with hegel-core 0.2.3 (verified via stdio-probe.scm).

title: Implement channel multiplexing layer (channel.scm)
description: New module src/hegel/channel.scm implementing multiplexed channels over a single connection. Control channel (id 0) for handshake and run_test. Client channels (odd ids) for test execution. Request/reply with message IDs.
close_reason: Implemented: src/hegel/channel.scm with make-hegel-channel, channel-send-raw!/recv-raw!, channel-send-cbor!/recv-cbor!, channel-send-request!, channel-close!. 16 tests pass. NOTE: live probe revealed that a packet multiplexer is needed for interleaved traffic — filed as hegel-guile-10.

title: C9 gap: run_test requires channel_id at top level
description: Live probe against hegel-core 0.2.3 showed run_test command must include 'channel_id' (the client-created test channel ID) and 'test_cases' at top level, not nested in settings. Current msg-run-test wraps test_cases in settings.max_examples. Must match: {command: run_test, channel_id: N, test_cases: N}. Server uses message['channel_id'] to register_client_channel().

title: C9 gap: status strings must be UPPERCASE (VALID/INVALID/INTERESTING)
description: Live probe confirmed hegel-core uses Python Status enum names: VALID, INVALID, INTERESTING (uppercase). Current protocol.scm uses lowercase strings. mark_complete status field must use uppercase. Server does Status[message['status']] which is case-sensitive. Also: 'passed'/'failed' mapping is wrong — the actual statuses are VALID (not passed), INTERESTING (not failed).

title: C9 gap: need packet multiplexer for interleaved channel traffic
description: Live probe showed packets from different channels arrive interleaved on the shared socket. Current channel.scm reads sequentially from the port — channel-recv-reply! may read a packet meant for a different channel. Need a connection-level multiplexer (connection-mux) that reads packets and buffers them per channel ID. Without this, concurrent test cases and server-initiated events cannot be handled. This is the most significant remaining architecture gap.

title: C9 gap: reply payload must be {result: value} map, not bare CBOR
description: hegel-core channel.py PendingRequest.get() does cbor2.loads(payload) then checks 'error' in payload and returns payload['result']. Our channel.scm sends bare CBOR values as replies. Must wrap in {result: value} for success or {error: msg, type: name} for errors. Applies to all reply packets (acks to test_case events, mark_complete replies, etc).

title: C9 gap: server drives test case lifecycle, not client
description: The actual protocol flow: server sends {event: test_case, channel_id: N} as a REQUEST to the client on the test channel. Server creates even-numbered channels for each test case. Client must: (1) receive the test_case event, (2) reply with {result: null}, (3) send generate commands on the server-created channel, (4) send mark_complete. Current test.scm drives from the client side (counting to N). Must invert to server-driven event loop.

title: Verify schema type vocabulary against hegel-core from_schema()
description: Conjecture: schema type names need updating. Live probe returned 'Unsupported schema: {type: integers}'. Need to read hegel-core's schema.py from_schema() to determine actual vocabulary. Previous EVV (hegel-guile-5) suggested singular forms (integer, boolean, float, string, list). Verify and update generators.scm accordingly.

title: C4 CONFIRMED: hegel-core 0.2.3 ships --stdio flag
description: hegel-core 0.2.3 (installed via uv tool install) includes --stdio flag for stdin/stdout protocol communication. No upstream patch needed. This confirms C4 (stdio transport viable) and simplifies the architecture — no socket path coordination required for stdio mode. server.scm should support both socket and stdio modes.

title: E2E hangs at 200+ test cases — mux buffering issue at scale
description: Live E2E against hegel-core 0.2.3 passes at 10 test cases but hangs at 200+. Likely cause: StopTest reply on the test_case channel is not consumed by the mux after mark_complete, causing the mux to block on the next mux-read-for-channel! waiting for a packet on the test channel while the unconsumed StopTest packet sits in the buffer for the test_case channel. Hypothesis may also send additional packets during shrinking phases. Investigation: add packet-level tracing to the mux to identify which read is blocking and what packets are buffered.

title: examples/shape-coercion.scm — vector/list defensive coercion cage
description: Property cage for the as-list pattern: a defensive helper that coerces vector | list | nil | #f to a canonical list. Origin: ported from guile-sage src/sage/util.scm:as-list (commit dsp-dr/guile-sage@15c7960, bd guile-p94). The bug it caught: ollama-parse-tool-call hard-coded vector? but the JSON parser returned lists, silently dropping all native tool_calls. The properties pin: list identity, vector roundtrip, empty cases, and cardinality preservation across input shapes.
close_reason: Landed as examples/shape-coercion.scm with 4 properties (~750 trials total).

title: examples/json-int-fuzz.scm — coerce->int input-shape fuzzing cage
description: Property cage for the coerce->int pattern: any tool that takes a JSON-encoded integer arg must survive 7+ input shapes (int, string-int, float, string-float, garbage, #f, '()) without throwing. Origin: ported from guile-sage src/sage/tools.scm:coerce->int (commit dsp-dr/guile-sage@769da88, bd guile-bcy). Includes the (integer? 20.0) Guile gotcha as a teaching moment — the naive fast-path fails on inexact integers.
close_reason: Landed as examples/json-int-fuzz.scm with 6 properties (~2300 trials total).

title: examples/file-roundtrip.scm — write_file path-honesty cage
description: Property cage for the resolve-path / write_file honesty pattern: any filesystem helper must report the ACTUAL location it wrote to, not the input path the user supplied. Origin: ported from guile-sage src/sage/tools.scm:resolve-path (commit dsp-dr/guile-sage@728bcc4, bd guile-ecn). Properties: write_file echoes a path that exists, write->read roundtrip preserves content, byte-count claim matches actual file size, relative paths land under workspace.
close_reason: Landed as examples/file-roundtrip.scm with 4 properties (~350 trials total).

title: fix(.beads): dolt database name 'beads_hegel-guile' contains a dash, blocks bd init
description: bd init fails with 'invalid database name: "beads_hegel-guile"' because Dolt rejects dashes in database names. Currently bd cannot operate on this repo's beads dir at all — issues are only writeable directly via .beads/issues.jsonl. Fix: edit .beads/metadata.json to use 'beads_hegel_guile' (underscore), then bd init recreates the dolt store from the JSONL. Discovered while cross-pollinating sage's PBT cages back into hegel-guile examples.
